posts.text:
Legally, the DOJ is clearly violating the Epstein Transparency Act. 

Not a close call:
- Production did not meet the deadline. 
- There are about 4k new Fri files - a paltry portion of the total. (Takes less than a month to redact those)
- Most other docs were already public

posts.text:
New: The Senate agrees to pass the House bill to release the Epstein Files. 

Congress has voted so overwhelmingly that this is now a veto-proof majority. The President has lost.

posts.text:
So in just the past 3 weeks:

Trump DOJ lost James Comey case

and lost Letitia James case

Trump lost bid to stop the Epstein files vote 

Trump lost every GOP senator on that vote 

Trump also lost bid to stop release of 20,000 Epstein emails

MTG resigned 

GOP lost 2025 elections

posts.text: President Trump’s DOJ is being pressed for hiding more Epstein documents. Top House Oversight lawmakers met with embattled Attorney General Pam Bondi and Deputy AG Todd Blanche over hiding files and alleged abuse of redactions.
posts.quote: President Trump’s DOJ is being pressed for hiding more Epstein documents. Top House Oversight lawmakers met with embattled Attorney General Pam Bondi and Deputy AG Todd Blanche over hiding files and alleged abuse of redactions.

epstein-scandal.quote:
The DOJ's Epstein Files reveal new details about *Epstein's death* - and the DOJ's own misleading changes to an FBI account of a possible inmate near Epstein's cell the night he died.

Our report and timeline:
piped.video/QMaM5uw0jJ0?si=icY1…

Legally, the DOJ is clearly violating the Epstein Transparency Act. 

Not a close call:
- Production did not meet the deadline. 
- There are about 4k new Fri files - a paltry portion of the total. (Takes less than a month to redact those)
- Most other docs were already public

New: The Senate agrees to pass the House bill to release the Epstein Files. 

Congress has voted so overwhelmingly that this is now a veto-proof majority. The President has lost.

President Trump’s DOJ is being pressed for hiding more Epstein documents. Top House Oversight lawmakers met with embattled Attorney General Pam Bondi and Deputy AG Todd Blanche over hiding files and alleged abuse of redactions.
